rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSEMARY ANNE GILBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the extraordinary fees requested are reasonable ($10,362.50 to the attorney and $2,750.00 to the executor) and if a $5,000 reserve is really necessary, when Executor has reportedly already filed all income tax returns that were due and paid all taxes, and there should no more tax on the less than $73,000 on hand in a checking account (unless the checking account generated more than $600 in annual gross income). 1) Most of the time entries shown in the attorney fee declaration are vague or appear to be for ordinary services. 2) If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. 3) The second codicil to the will gave Executor $10,000 to carry out the testator's wishes, which included finding a home for the pets (Paragraph Fourth) and contacting the Neptune Society where Decedent already had arrangements for cremation (Paragraph Ninth). 4) Executor already distributed $10,000 to herself and $5,000 to Robin Winburn. Probate Code Section 10520(c) requires Notice of Proposed Action to be given before cash to general pecuniary devisees is distributed, not exceeding $10,000 to any one person. 5) Any reserve set aside cannot be used to pay additional attorney fees, which are included in the statutory fee. 6) The omnibus clause requests that any other property be distributed to the SFSPCA, but the second codicil added a provision that, in the event that any of Decedent's accounts "may not be eligible to be distributed to any corporate organization or non-profit, and only distributed to a natural person...the rest and remainder... that cannot be distributed as a result in limitations on distributions from any IRA accounts to Shan Xiao." Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEATRICE POWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared: (1) Notice of Hearing failed to affirm that this is a report of the status of a decedent's estate administration made under section 12200 on the first page. Prob. Code sec. 12201(b). (2) Ex parte application (drop-off) to increase bond must be submitted. CRC 7.204. I&As show the assets of the estate exceed the current bond amount. It is the attorney's duty to immediately request an increase in the bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONARD RUSSELL MORECI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of filing a petition for final distribution or a second report of status with a petition requesting additional time to administer the estate will be set for 11/18/25. No appearance will be required on that date if a petition is filed by 11/4/25. NB: An amended petition completely restates and supersedes the petition for all purposes [CRC 7.3(3)]; there is no need to refer to the petition, especially to "supplement" it when the amended petition amends the entire pleading. Attorney costs should be fully itemized, showing what was filed that incurred filing or messenger fees, etc. Please be careful with the title of pleadings and references to the correct title of the pleading in the proposed order, etc. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA M. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the date of death was 1/10/17, the amended petition must be amended, not just "supplemented." [CRC 7.3.] (2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) What is Christina Colon's address? She must be given notice as the successor-in-interest to Irene Figueroa. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/23/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE JESUS O. PULE AND AMALIA PULE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition (1) For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration; 2) For An Accounting 3) For An Order To Distribute Trust Assets; 4) For Damages Due To
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. The pleading form of proof of service filed on 7/17/25 was apparently for the "Notice of Appearance," which is not a requirement for Probate matters, in any event, and was not the proper method for proving that notice was mailed to all of the interested parties using the mandatory Judicial Council form. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The petition has not been reviewed, as no courtesy copy has been received, but it appears, upon a cursory skim, that there are multiple beneficiaries whose whereabouts are unknown. If the whereabouts of interested parties are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing efforts made to locate the persons entitled to notice. If, after diligent search, such persons cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice to such persons be dispensed with. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/23/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD PARKER JOHNSTON, JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents, grandparents, and uncles/aunts and when did they die? (3) If cousins survived, item 6f of the petition is not the first box that applies. (4) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. (5) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/9/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A clearer, more legible proposed order would be helpful.)
